sourceMovie.title: The Devil's Own
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Devil's Own (1997) as a crime theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The crime and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Panic Room
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Devil's Own clicked for you, Panic Room (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Devil's Own?
faq.answer: Try Panic Room, A Walk Among the Tombstones, Colombiana—each shares DNA with The Devil's Own while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Devil's Own?
faq.answer: The Devil's Own (1997) is best described as Crime, Thriller, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Devil's Own worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
